output_text: A 7-year-old British schoolgirl, India Mayhew, has died after being thrown from a horse while on holiday in India with her family. The incident occurred at a riding facility in Matheran, 20 miles east of Mumbai, when the horse suddenly bolted and dragged her a distance, causing serious head injuries. Her parents, who were also on the holiday, chose not to press charges against the operator, stating it was an accident. The police have registered a case of accidental death, and the British Deputy High Commission in Mumbai is providing consular assistance to the family.

Austrian designers Maya Pindeus and Johanna Pichlbauer have created a robot called the "Beautification machine" that applies makeup, specifically eyeliner and lipstick, to a person's face. The device consists of three components: a lipstick wheel, an eyeliner applicator, and a mask that illuminates when the process is complete. While the machine is intended to simplify the makeup application process, it appears to have some precision issues, with the eyeliner applicator sometimes applying random strokes. The machine was showcased at the Biennale Internationale Design Saint-Etienne 2015 in France as part of the designers' art project.

A former US Army enlistee, John T. Booker Jr., was arrested and charged with attempting to detonate a car bomb at Fort Riley military base in Kansas. Booker, who also used the alias Mohammed Abdullah Hassan, had posted on Facebook about the "adrenaline rush" of dying in jihad and had expressed plans to commit an insider attack against American soldiers. The "bomb" he attempted to detonate was actually a non-explosive device created by confidential informants. Booker's enlistment was terminated after he waived his Miranda rights and confessed to the FBI about his plans to engage in violent jihad in support of ISIS.

England's push for victory in the first Test against the West Indies was led by Gary Ballance, who scored his fourth Test century in just nine matches. However, the spotlight shifted to Chris Jordan, who made a stunning catch to dismiss Darren Bravo, leaving West Indies at 98 for two and 340 runs short of their target. England's Alastair Cook set a challenging target of 438 for the West Indies, but they began their chase solidly, reaching 90 for one before Jordan's catch. England remains firm favorites to secure a valuable victory, but all results are still possible.

Massachusetts Senator Elizabeth Warren expressed relief that Dzhokhar Tsarnaev has been found guilty of the Boston bombing, but does not support the death penalty. Warren believes Tsarnaev should spend his life in jail without the possibility of parole, stating that he should "die in prison." This stance aligns with her long-held anti-death penalty views. The case will now move to the penalty stage of the trial, where the jury will decide whether to sentence Tsarnaev to life in prison or the death penalty.

Tokyo's Narita Airport has installed brightly-colored running tracks in its new Terminal 3 to celebrate the 2020 Olympics. The cushioned tracks, which include directional information, serve as a useful navigation tool for passengers, with blue lanes leading to departures and red lanes to arrivals. The airport opted for the running tracks to save money, instead of installing traditional moving walkways or illuminated signs. The new terminal, primarily used for low-cost carrier departures, first opened on April 8.

Hull City manager Steve Bruce is interested in signing Leeds United midfielder Alex Mowatt, who has impressed this season with nine goals. Mowatt, a 20-year-old England U20 international, has drawn attention from several Premier League clubs and was scouted by Hull during a recent match against Wolves. Bruce is keen to bolster his squad with homegrown talent, and Mowatt is one of several promising Leeds players who could be targeted by other clubs in the summer. Mowatt graduated from Leeds' academy in 2013 and has since become a key player for the first team.

Parma, a Serie A club, has been deducted four points by the Italian Football Federation (FIGC) for breaching financial regulations. This adds to the three points they were previously penalized, leaving them with a total of 12 points from 30 games. The club is currently at the bottom of Serie A and has been struggling financially, with players not being paid all season. Parma was also declared bankrupt last month.

Emily Grimson, a 25-year-old model from Shropshire, has built a lucrative career as a hand model, earning up to £100 an hour. Despite being one of Britain's most successful models, her face is rarely seen, as her hands are in high demand for product videos, TV advertisements, and magazine ads. To maintain her perfect hands, Emily moisturizes them up to ten times a day and follows a strict beauty routine. She has been modeling full-time since autumn and is signed with BMA Models.

Northern Ireland's tourism board is responding to comments made by actor Kit Harington, who joked that Belfast is "wonderful for two or three days" during an appearance on a US late-night chat show. Harington, who plays Jon Snow in Game of Thrones, has spent five years filming in Belfast, which is a principal location for the show. Despite his lighthearted jabs, Belfast has become a popular city break destination in the UK, thanks in part to the show's popularity. Tourism bosses have leveraged the show's success to attract visitors to the city.

Burnley manager Sean Dyche has encouraged striker Danny Ings to focus on enjoying his game, rather than putting too much pressure on himself to score. Ings has not found the net in seven appearances, but Dyche believes that if he starts to play with a smile, his goals will follow. Despite struggling for goals, Burnley has collected five points from a tough run of eight games, including victories over Manchester City and draws with Chelsea and Tottenham. Dyche is confident that his forwards will come good again and help the team avoid relegation.

Misty Machinshok, 33, has been sentenced to 15 to 30 years in prison for convincing her husband to repeatedly rape their 15-year-old daughter in order to conceive another child. Machinshok, who is no longer fertile, coached the pair on the best positions to conceive and even held her daughter's hand during the initial rapes. Her husband, Gary Machinshok, 29, has also pleaded no contest to raping the child and will be sentenced separately. Both victims are now in foster care, and the case has been described as one of the most heinous crimes a prosecutor has dealt with in 14 years.

Freddie Roach, trainer of Manny Pacquiao, believes Floyd Mayweather does not deserve to be considered the best fighter of their generation. Roach rates super-middleweight star Andre Ward and middleweight sensation Gennady Golovkin above Mayweather due to his perceived lack of competition and "ducking" of opponents. Roach thinks Mayweather has picked and chosen his fights, which is why he doesn't consider him the best. He is confident that Pacquiao will spring a surprise in the upcoming welterweight showdown against Mayweather.

Here's a summary of the article in 3-4 sentences:

Nathan Brown, a 19-year-old apprentice electrician, died after being electrocuted while testing lights at a factory in Rochdale, Greater Manchester. He fell 12 feet from a ladder onto a toilet block roof after apparently touching exposed electrical bars powering a crane, causing a fatal shock. Nathan was working with his father, an experienced electrician, at Eurofabs UK Ltd when the tragedy occurred in September 2013. He had been training to follow in his father's footsteps and was conducting a routine electrical inspection at the time of his death.

Scientists at Stanford University have created super-strong robots inspired by geckos, which can carry over 100 times their own weight. The robots, weighing as little as 9 grams, can haul heavy loads up vertical walls, with one model, μTug, able to drag a weight 2000 times heavier. The robots' "feet" are covered in tiny rubber spikes that mimic geckos' small hairs, allowing them to create a weak bond with the surface and climb up walls. These robots have potential applications in emergencies, factories, and construction sites.

Jarret Stoll, a 32-year-old hockey player for the L.A. Kings, was arrested on a drug-possession charge at a Las Vegas resort on Friday. He was charged with possession of cocaine and ecstasy and released on $5,000 bail. The Kings organization has launched an internal investigation and is withholding further comment until more information is gathered. Stoll has been with the Kings since 2008 and is reportedly in a relationship with TV personality Erin Andrews.
